perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms and conditions set forth herein, Theismann hereby grants to Bizzingo and its affiliates the unlimited right and privilege during the Term (as defined herein) and within the Territory to use the Property (as defined herein) in connection with the advertisement, promotion, and sale of the Network in the Territory whether through film, television, radio, print and Internet media, including the right to use the Property in or on the Network.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms and conditions set forth herein, Theismann hereby grants to Bizzingo and its affiliates the right and privilege during the Term (as defined herein) and within the Territory to use the Property (as defined herein) in connection with the advertisement, promotion, and sale of the Network in the Territory whether through film, television, radio, print and Internet media, including the right to use the Property in or on the Network, provided Bizzingo obtains prior written consent from Theismann for each individual use of the Property.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'unlimited right' to a right requiring 'prior written consent from Theismann for each individual use of the Property', this creates an in-text contradiction with the later clause in Section 1 where it states 'Theismann agrees that such material, submitted for approval as provided herein may be deemed by Bizzingo to have been approved hereunder if the same is not disapproved by the Theismann in writing within fourteen (14) days after Theismann's receipt thereof.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Theismann agrees that such material, submitted for approval as provided herein may be deemed by Bizzingo to have been approved hereunder if the same is not disapproved by the Theismann in writing within fourteen (14) days after Theismann's receipt thereof. Theismann agrees that any material submitted hereunder will not be unreasonably disapproved and, if it is disapproved, that Theismann shall advise Bizzingo in writing of the specific grounds therefor at the time of disapproval and provide Bizzingo with specific changes or alterations to such materials. Bizzingo thereafter shall have the right and privileged to use such Property as provided in this Agreement subject to such changes or alterations.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted 'unlimited right' to Bizzingo to use the property. The changed text restricts this right by requiring 'prior written consent from Theismann for each individual use of the Property.' This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that approval can be 'deemed' if not disapproved within 14 days. The change introduces a significant inconsistency in the approval process, potentially causing confusion and disputes, as one section requires proactive consent for each use, while the other allows for deemed approval based on inaction. This falls under the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' where the obligations are defined inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: In addition, concurrent with the execution hereof, Bizzingo will pay Theismann the Initial Bonus described in Schedule A.
perturbation.changed_text: In addition, concurrent with the execution hereof, Bizzingo *may* pay Theismann the Initial Bonus described in Schedule A, subject to Bizzingo's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will pay' to 'may pay, subject to Bizzingo's sole discretion' introduces a contradiction. The original text implies an obligation to pay the bonus, while the changed text makes it discretionary. This contradicts Schedule A, Section II, Paragraph 2, where it states 'Concurrent with the execution hereof, Bizzingo will issue Theismann, as a one time bonus, a common stock purchase warrant to purchase 1,000,000 shares of common stock of Bizzingo'.
perturbation.contradicted_location: SCHEDULE A (Attached to and made a part of the Celebrity Endorsement Agreement dated March 14, 2012 by and between Bizzingo, Inc. and Joseph Theismann) II
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Initial Bonus. Concurrent with the execution hereof, Bizzingo will issue Theismann, as a one time bonus, a common stock purchase warrant to purchase 1,000,000 shares of common stock of Bizzingo at a purchase price of $0.15 per share during a term of five (5) years. The form of warrant is attached hereto as Schedule A-II and shall be referred to as the "Bonus Warrant."
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Bizzingo 'will pay' Theismann the Initial Bonus. The changed text alters this to 'may pay, subject to Bizzingo's sole discretion,' which introduces an ambiguity and renders the bonus payment discretionary instead of obligatory. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states 'Bizzingo will issue Theismann...a common stock purchase warrant,' establishing a clear obligation. The 'may pay' language directly conflicts with the 'will issue' language, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction perturbation type. The change makes the obligation uncertain where certainty previously existed.

perturbation.original_text: Bizzingo agrees to defend, indemnify, and hold Theismann harmless against all costs, expenses, and losses (including reasonable attorney fees and costs) incurred through claims of third parties against Theismann based on the manufacture or sale of the Network including, but not limited to, actions founded on network liability.
perturbation.changed_text: Bizzingo agrees to defend, indemnify, and hold Theismann harmless against all *direct* costs, expenses, and losses (including reasonable attorney fees and costs) *directly* incurred through claims of third parties *demonstrably caused* by Theismann's promotion, endorsement or image used in conjuction to the manufacture or sale of the Network including, but not limited to, actions founded on network liability.
perturbation.explanation: By restricting the indemnification to direct costs 'demonstrably caused' by Theismann's promotion or endorsement of the product creates a contradiction when considering Bizzingo's responsibilities in relation to the overall Network liability. Section 9(d) states that Bizzingo agrees to defend, indemnify, and hold Theismann harmless against all costs incurred through claims of third parties against Theismann based on the manufacture or sale of the Network including, but not limited to, actions founded on network liability. By adding the qualifier, we create a contradiction, and the extent of Bizzingo's responsibilities and the circumstances in which they are applicable becomes ambiguous. The original clause indicates coverage for any claim against Theismann concerning the sale or manufacture of the Network, while the revision would significantly narrow that coverage, potentially leaving Theismann exposed to actions founded on network liability that are not demonstrably a direct result of their actions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Bizzingo agrees to defend, indemnify, and hold Theismann harmless against all costs, expenses, and losses (including reasonable attorney fees and costs) incurred through claims of third parties against Theismann based on the manufacture or sale of the Network including, but not limited to, actions founded on network liability. In this regarding, within thirty (30) days from the execution of this Agreement, Bizzingo will secure an insurance policy with limits of $5,000,000 per event and $ 5,000,000 umbrella, naming Theismann as an additional insured, covering the losses and claims stated in this sub-paragraph d.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant contradiction by limiting the indemnification to 'direct costs demonstrably caused' by Theismann's actions. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states Bizzingo will defend and indemnify Theismann against 'all costs' related to claims based on the manufacture or sale of the Network, including actions founded on network liability. The addition of the qualifier significantly narrows Bizzingo's responsibilities, creating a clear inconsistency in the scope of indemnification and thus a strong, impactful contradiction.
